title: drosera anglica var. woodii
intro: Drosera anglica var. woodii, also known as the English sundew, is a carnivorous plant species that belongs to the Droseraceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as coughs and bronchitis, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Drosera anglica var. woodii include relieving congestion, soothing sore throats, and reducing inflammation in the body. The plant can be prepared into herbal remedies such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are typically used to alleviate respiratory symptoms and promote overall well-being.
